semantic.role: CI/CD integration template for automated architecture validation on pull requests
responsibilities: Providing a reusable GitHub Actions workflow for ArchSpine
Defining steps to install ArchSpine, sync spine data, and run checks
Automatically posting PR comments when architecture violations are detected
Guiding users on setup prerequisites (spine init, LLM_API_KEY secret)
outOfScope: Configuration of ArchSpine rules or policies

English.purpose: This document is a GitHub Actions workflow template that enables automated architecture governance within pull request workflows. It defines a complete pipeline to install ArchSpine, synchronize project state, run architecture checks, and report violations as PR comments, ensuring architectural rules are enforced before merging.
English.context_and_audience: Designed for development teams using GitHub and ArchSpine who want to integrate architecture validation into their CI pipeline. The intended audience includes DevOps engineers, platform engineers, and developers responsible for maintaining architectural standards in a repository. Readers should already have initialized ArchSpine (spine init) and configured their project with appropriate rules.
key_takeaways: Automates architecture checks on every pull request synchronize/reopen event
Requires an LLM_API_KEY secret to be set in the repository for LLM-based analysis
Fails the workflow if violations are found, preventing non-compliant merges
Updates existing bot comments rather than creating duplicate comments
